ground_truth.chief_complaint: Deep hand laceration with fever and rigors

symptoms.name: wound redness and warmth
symptoms.detail: skin around the cut is red and warm

relevant_history: Tetanus immunization status unknown - to be checked in records (stated turns 6-7)

vitals.blood_pressure: stated as still to be checked
vitals.note: Respiratory rate and SpO2 were not discussed in the conversation

diagnosis.primary: Hand laceration with possible wound infection - nurse links the fever, fast heart rate and redness (turn 9); not a confirmed diagnosis

ordered_tests: Blood pressure (nurse states she will check it)
Doctor assessment (nurse states a doctor will see the patient)

triage_decision.urgency: Expedited - nurse decides the patient should be seen by a doctor 'fairly soon' rather than wait a long time (turn 9)
triage_decision.rationale: Fever 38.6 C, heart rate 104 bpm, and local wound redness (stated by nurse)

follow_up: Check tetanus status in records (stated turn 7)
Doctor assessment of the wound (stated turn 9)

clinical_note.chief_complaint: Deep hand laceration ~3h ago from broken glass, with fever and rigors since the afternoon.
clinical_note.history: 27M, asthma (per context). Pain 4/10 at wound. Reports shivering and feeling feverish. Skin around the cut red and warm. Tetanus status unknown.
clinical_note.vitals: Temperature 38.6 C, heart rate 104 bpm; blood pressure still to be taken.
clinical_note.impression: Hand laceration with possible wound infection; nurse expedites physician assessment.
